items.title: Labourer in Kelantan gets four years’ jail, RM6,000 fine for abducting man over brother’s debt and stealing Volkswagen Jetta
items.summary: He was charged under Section 365 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same Code, which carries a penalty of up to seven years’ imprisonment and a fine. The court sentenced the accused to four years’ jail from the date of his arrest on June 16, along with a RM3,000 fine for the kidnapping offence, with an additional three months’ jail if the fine is not paid. However, the court sentenced him to three years’ jail and a RM3,000 fine, with an additional three months’ imprisonment if the fine is unpaid.

items.title: Elderly man takes wife on trishaw ride for glimpse of PM
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: The scorching heat did not deter an 86-year-old man from pedalling his trishaw for nearly a kilometre to fulfil his wife's wish of seeing Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim up close during his breakfast stop at a coffee shop in Senggarang here today. Abdul Razak Pelangga, 87, said he decided to take his wife, Jamilah Samsudin, 64, on the trishaw from their home in Kampung Parit Bugis to Kedai Kopi Hailam Sri Medan after learning yesterday that the prime minister would be visiting the small town. "Many years ago, before he became prime minister, he came to perform prayers at our mosque.

items.title: PH grades Johor seats for election strategy
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) is classifying all 56 Johor seats based on priority levels as part of its election strategy, according to current ground support trends. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the "grading" system allows the coalition to focus resources on key constituencies with different voter profiles and challenges. Also present was PH Puteri Wangsa candidate Dr Maszlee Malik.

items.title: Govt to monitor Budi Diesel based on data, feedback - Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The federal government will continuously assess the implementation of the Budi Diesel initiative based on data collected on the ground, including usage patterns and feedback from stakeholders, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. Responding to a question on whether there is a review period to reassess the quota structure after five days of rollout on July 1, he said the approach remains flexible and data-driven, with adjustments made based on actual usage patterns. For example, under Budi95, the original quota was 300 litres, but it was later revised to 200 litres based on usage patterns.

items.title: Two schoolboys arrested after ramming cop at Seremban roadblock
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Police arrested two schoolboys after they rammed a policeman while trying to escape a roadblock during Op Samseng Jalanan at Taman Panchor Jaya yesterday. Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the teenagers, aged 14 and 15, were riding a Honda EX5 motorcycle and were detained at 6.45pm. "The duo acted aggressively by trying to escape and rammed a policeman, causing bruises to his head, right ankle, right hand, and waist.

items.title: Mother, two children arrested for posing as welfare officers, duping elderly woman
items.summary: IPOH: Police have arrested a mother and her two children for allegedly cheating a 67-year-old woman out of jewellery worth about RM8,000 by posing as welfare officers. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the victim was waiting for a medical check-up at a hospital in Teluk Intan on June 29 when she was approached by a woman claiming to be a welfare officer. Alwi said the suspect allegedly offered the victim RM2,000 a month in welfare assistance before asking her to hand over the jewellery she was wearing, purportedly as part of the application process.

items.title: Anwar: Certain political parties uneasy over my firm anti-corruption stance
items.summary: The Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman said the Madani government's uncompromising approach towards those who steal public funds or abuse their positions for personal gain had made certain quarters uneasy. They know we safeguard everything," he said while speaking at the PMX Meet-and-Greet programme for the Senggarang state constituency in conjunction with the Johor state election here today. He said that despite making numerous visits to Johor as prime minister, he had never taken any land, projects or shares in the state.

items.title: Muda says PH out of touch for omitting flood issues from manifesto
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Muda has questioned Pakatan Harapan's (PH) manifesto, claiming it makes no reference to flooding, climate change or environmental issues affecting the state. Its Puteri Wangsa candidate Rashifa Aljunied said the omission reflected a lack of understanding of Johor voters' priorities, particularly as floods have become more frequent and severe. "If the PH manifesto truly understood the voice of the people of Johor, they would realise that floods have caused significant losses to residents.

items.title: Move over Musang King because Mardi is prepping three top-secret premium durian hybrids
items.summary: KUALA KANGSAR, July 5 — The Malaysian Agricultural Research and Development Institute (Mardi) has planted about 25,000 trees of the MRDI Super 88 (MS88) hybrid durian nationwide to date. “The MS88 is one of three premium durian varieties developed by Mardi since the early 2000s, together with MDUR78 and MDUR79, which remain popular among durian enthusiasts,” he told reporters after the Duriotourism Mardi Kuala Kangsar programme with the media at the Mardi Agrotechnology Park here today. In another development, Mohamad Zabawi said Mardi is selecting three new hybrid durian varieties with potential to be introduced as premium types to meet industry demand and consumer preference.

items.title: Defence Ministry will not allow KDA talks to delay RM1.06b NSM claim
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace AS (KDA) has requested talks with the Defence Ministry to resolve a RM1.06 billion claim following the cancellation of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system for the Royal Malaysian Navy. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, however, stressed that the ministry will not allow the talks to be used as a tactic to delay the claims settlement process. Following the cancellation, Malaysia filed a RM1.06 billion claim against KDA, which includes a direct claim of €129.86 million (about RM604 million) for payments already made by the government.

items.title: Anwar takes swipe at Onn Hafiz over federal-state meeting attendance
items.summary: SEGAMAT: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has taken a swipe at caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, questioning his absence from federal–state coordination meetings. Anwar said cooperation between Putrajaya and state governments is essential for the benefit of the people. The prime minister also said he would convene a meeting with all menteri besar, chief ministers, and the Sarawak Premier within the next month to discuss development issues, including matters involving Felda settlers.

items.title: Mohamad Hasan hints BN could shake up ‘traditional seats’ for Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 5 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is likely to make changes by no longer retaining “traditional seats” in the division of constituencies contested by the coalition in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the move was due to the composition of voters in most constituencies having changed significantly. That is my view as the Negeri Sembilan BN chairman: do a seat swap because we must give voters an opportunity.

items.title: Synthetic drugs account for 83pct of drug abuse cases nationwide
items.summary: LABUAN: Synthetic drugs account for 83 per cent of drug abuse cases recorded in the country, prompting the National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada) to closely monitor the emerging trend involving fentanyl, its director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said. He said methamphetamine remained the most commonly abused synthetic drug, while the remaining 17 per cent of cases involved traditional drugs such as cannabis and heroin. He said the test strips available to Nada could detect several substances, including amphetamine-type stimulants, or methamphetamine, fentanyl, AB-PINACA, cocaine and ketamine.

items.title: Anwar summons Felda management tomorrow to resolve settlers' long-standing issues
items.summary: SEGAMAT: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will summon the Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) management to the Prime Minister's Office tomorrow. This is part of efforts to resolve long-standing issues affecting settlers, particularly those involving second-generation land ownership and housing. The Pakatan Harapan chairman said his administration had already adopted a policy to ensure that second-generation Felda settlers are guaranteed land for housing.

items.title: MACC denies man in viral social media post is its officer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has denied claims that a man featured in a viral social media post is one of its officers. "Verify the identity of MACC officers through official channels before providing any information or cooperation. "Report any suspected impersonation incidents to the MACC immediately.

items.title: Fahmi: Johor among priorities as MCMC moves to improve internet coverage with shared networks
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 5 — The Communications Ministry, through the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), plans to expand the implementation of the Multi-Operator Core Network (MOCN) nationwide to improve connectivity in areas with limited internet coverage. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative would enable telecommunications companies to collaborate, particularly in extending coverage to rural, remote and underserved areas with weak signal reception. “In Johor, we are currently identifying several areas for implementation.

items.title: Khaled Nordin says Hobart Camp blast investigation report will be submitted by mid‑month
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 5 — The investigation report into the grenade explosion that claimed the lives of two military personnel during a training exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun last month is expected to be submitted to the Ministry of Defence by the middle of this month. In the incident at about 10.57am on June 16, the two personnel involved — Corporal Norazmi Abu Bakar of the Sixth Battalion, Royal Malay Regiment (RAMD), and Private Siti Khadijah Sungip of the First Squadron, Royal Engineers Regiment (RAJD) — sustained severe injuries in the explosion during the training exercise. Two weeks later, on June 29, another incident occurred at the same camp when a firearm discharged during a combat enhancement training (LPT) exercise, injuring two military personnel.

items.title: Main puteri, menora return to public stage as Kelantan tourism assets
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: Traditional performing arts once banned from public viewing in Kelantan are making a comeback through a cultural tourism initiative led by the National Department for Culture and Arts. Its director-general, Mohd Amran Mohd Haris, said the revived performances include main puteri, menora and makyong. Also present were Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud and Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry secretary-general Datuk Shaharuddin Abu Sohot.

items.title: Defence Ministry wary of delay tactics as naval supplier seeks talks over cancelled RM1.06b deal, says minister
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 5 — The Defence Ministry has stated that the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) supplier has requested discussions over the RM1.06 billion claim stemming from the cancelled procurement for the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN). However, Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry is cautious about any discussion being used as an excuse or tactic to delay the claims process. Following the cancellation, Malaysia is claiming RM1.06 billion from KDA, comprising EUR129.86 million (RM604 million) in direct claims for payments already made, plus EUR96.26 million (RM448.12 million) in indirect claims covering cost implications and knock-on effects from the failed supply.

items.title: From shopping carts to prayer mats: Minister wants Friday prayers expanded to malls nationwide
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, July 5 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan has welcomed states across the country to hold Friday prayers at shopping malls, following the positive response to the initiative at several locations in the Federal Territories. Zulkifli said the initiative was first introduced in the Federal Territories through a collaboration between the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) and the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP), as it falls under the jurisdiction of the two agencies. “So far, Friday prayers have been held at several shopping malls, including IOI City Mall, Alamanda Shopping Centre and Suria KLCC, while discussions are underway to expand the initiative to several other locations.

items.title: Kedah Sultan urges review of Penang lease payment, respect for state's constitutional rights
items.summary: ALOR STAR: The Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, has called on the federal government to review the lease payment for Penang based on current rates, saying the existing payment no longer reflects the spirit and original intent of the agreement. "I wish to remind all parties that the rights and sovereignty of the state of Kedah, as enshrined in the Federal Constitution of Malaysia, must be respected by all," he said at the 2026 Kedah State Awards and Honours Investiture Ceremony at Istana Anak Bukit today. His Highness said Kedah's rights and authority to manage and explore its natural resources must be respected and facilitated.

items.title: Three sons of Iran's slain leader Khamenei appear at funeral, not his successor
items.summary: TEHERAN: Three sons of slain Iranian leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei prayed beside his coffin and those of four other family members on Sunday, but Mojtaba, the son who has succeeded him as Iran's supreme leader, did not make an appearance. State TV showed Mostafa, Meysam and Masoud Khamenei praying behind the coffins laid out in the vast courtyard of Tehran's Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla, a sprawling religious complex. On Sunday, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf prayed behind the coffins.

items.title: MBSA speeds up 200m walkway near Stadium Shah Alam LRT3 station
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A section of the 200m pedestrian walkway linking the LRT3 Stadium Shah Alam station to a nearby traffic light junction is expected to be completed tomorrow, following public complaints over the lack of safe pedestrian access. He said the overall walkway project also includes an extension along Persiaran Akuatik, with construction on that section scheduled to begin on Tuesday. MBSA said it remained committed to providing safe, accessible public infrastructure.

items.title: MCMC to expand MOCN to improve internet connectivity in poor coverage areas
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: The Communications Ministry, through the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), plans to expand the implementation of the Multi-Operator Core Network (MOCN) nationwide to improve connectivity in areas with limited internet coverage. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative would enable telecommunications companies to collaborate, particularly in extending coverage to rural, remote and underserved areas with weak signal reception. "In Johor, we are currently identifying several areas for implementation.

items.title: Police: Shah Alam college failed to check employee’s criminal record before hiring him in teen molest, sodomy case
items.summary: “As a result, the college was unaware that the individual had a record involving sexual offences,” district police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said. Sarudin said police will record statements from the college’s management to assist the investigation. Sarudin said the suspect is currently remanded until July 11 and that police are awaiting medical reports.

items.title: BN's Onn Hafiz courts early security forces voters, seeks continued mandate [WATCH]
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has appealed to security forces in the state to support Barisan Nasional ahead of early voting for the polls on July 7. "I would like to record my highest appreciation and gratitude for your service, sacrifice and dedication in safeguarding the security and stability of Johor and the nation. "Leading and administering Johor is not an easy responsibility.

items.title: 12 undocumented migrants fined for illegal entry, overstaying in Kelantan
items.summary: PASIR MAS: Three women were among 12 undocumented migrants who were charged at the Sessions Court here today for illegal entry, overstaying and misuse of passes. The three women, comprising two Indonesians and one Thai national, were fined RM1,000, RM6,000 and RM10,000 respectively, in default of between one and three months' imprisonment, for offences involving illegal entry, overstaying and misuse of passes. Seven were charged with overstaying under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63.

items.title: Nearly 200,000 pick-up, jeep owners get extra Budi Diesel quota
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Nearly 200,000 owners of pick-up and jeep-type diesel vehicles nationwide have been approved for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month under the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) programme, the Finance Ministry (MOF) said. Meanwhile, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the additional allocation would help vehicle owners better manage their monthly diesel needs. For private diesel vehicles used by immediate family members who are not the registered owners, the original Budi Diesel recipient may apply to transfer eligibility through the Budi Madani portal, subject to the stipulated conditions and verification process.

items.title: Umno unfazed after Puad speaks at PH event, says Ahmad Maslan
items.summary: PONTIAN: Former Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi's attendance at a Pakatan Harapan event will not heavily impact the party, says Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan. The Johor Umno deputy chief said Puad was no longer an Umno member and was free to attend PH functions. Puad, who quit Umno last month, attended a PH programme at Felda Ulu Tebrau last night.

items.title: Full report on fatal Kem Hobart grenade blast expected by mid-month, says Khaled
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The full investigation report on a grenade explosion that killed two soldiers at the Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah is expected to be submitted to the Defence Ministry by mid-month. On whether existing standard operating procedures (SOPs) for training need to be reviewed, Khaled said the army has been directed to reassess its training approaches following two separate explosion incidents at the same camp. On June 16, two soldiers were killed in a grenade explosion during a training session at the Hobart Camp.

items.title: #NSTviral: Cops probe video of assault on teen at JB bus station
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police are investigating an assault on a teenager at a bus station here after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Johor Baru (South) police chief Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei said a policeman lodged a report over the altercation at 4.30pm on Friday. The incident had occurred earlier at 7.27am.

items.title: 32-year-old says she's often mistaken for 80-year-old husband's daughter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 48-year age gap often leads people to mistake 32-year-old Che Zainun Che Ahmad for the daughter of her 80-year-old husband, Hazlan Hajar. Che Zainun said she was initially uncomfortable with the assumptions but has since grown accustomed to them. I have also always preferred older men as partners," she said.

items.title: Xi ready to work with Kim for 'stable' China-N. Korea ties
items.summary: SEOUL: China's President Xi Jinping said he was ready to work with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to steer their bilateral relationship toward "long-term, sound and stable" development, Pyongyang's state media reported Sunday. In a message dated July 1, Xi thanked Kim for his congratulatory message marking the 105th anniversary of the founding of China's Communist Party, according to the official Korean Central News Agency (KCNA). Xi said Kim's message reflected the "deep and warm feelings" of the North Korean leader, the ruling Workers' Party and the North Korean people toward China.

items.title: Johor polls: Bukit Batu Pakatan candidate steps up campaign to lift voter turnout above 60pc
items.summary: KULAI, July 5 — With the Johor state election polling day just a few days away, incumbent Bukit Batu state assemblyman from Pakatan Harapan (PH), Arthur Chiong Sen Sern, is intensifying his campaign to ensure that the voter turnout on July 11 exceeds 60 per cent. “I and the (Bukit Batu PH) machinery are all out campaigning every day. N51 Bukit Batu: 49,963 voters 1.

items.title: China frees jailed pastor Ezra Jin
items.summary: BEIJING: China has released pastor Ezra Jin, the founder of an underground church who has been detained since October, his church confirmed to AFP on Sunday. Jin is the founder of Zion Church, one of China's unregistered churches that some Christians choose to worship at instead of state-sanctioned ones regulated by the government. US President Donald Trump raised his case when he met his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in May.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia and Singapore to launch digital immigration system and new lanes by January
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — Malaysia and Singapore are targeting the launch of a new digital immigration system and additional lanes by January next year to address congestion at border checkpoints more comprehensively. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiative will be jointly launched with his Singapore counterpart Lawrence Wong once all technical preparations and infrastructure are completed. “The Immigration Department will facilitate the movement, and once the system is ready, it will include new travel arrangements from Singapore to Johor Bahru,” he said.

items.title: Wedding trip ends as 2 men nabbed at roadblock with suspected ketum
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Two men's plan to attend a wedding in Kelantan ended when they were detained at a roadblock in Lalang Pepuyu near Tanah Merah yesterday. The men were detained by the General Operations Force (GOF) 9th Battalion under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 10.30pm after a greenish liquid, suspected to be ketum juice, weighing about one litre and worth RM30, was found in the vehicle they were travelling in. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said officers on duty at the Lalang Pepuyu roadblock spotted a Perodua Aruz carrying two men behaving suspiciously.

items.title: Anwar: DAP never disputes Malay rights and supports a government for all races
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that DAP has never disputed the rights of the Malays, thus refuting the narrative of certain quarters who are trying to portray the party as a racist organisation. Anwar, who is also the chairman of Pakatan Harapan (PH), said as proof, DAP has never objected to its intention to gazette 20.23 hectares of land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land. Poor Chinese should be helped, poor Indians should be helped, the majority of poor Malays should be helped,” he said at the Meeting Anwar Programme at a restaurant hall here today.

items.title: PM: New digital system in the works for Johor-Singapore border easing
items.summary: MUAR: A new digital system, to be launched jointly with Singapore in January next year, that is expected to ease congestion at the Johor-Singapore border. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, however, stopped short of revealing details of the initiative, saying doing so during the ongoing Johor election campaign could breach election regulations. He said traffic flow, border systems and congestion at the Johor-Singapore crossings had improved significantly over the past three years, adding that the new digital system would be launched together with Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong in January.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain to hit KL and several states until 4pm, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and eight states until 4pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the warning covers Kelantan (Jeli, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Besut, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong, Pekan and Rompin). The warning also applies to Selangor, involving the districts of Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang.

items.title: Police: College skipped background check on employee in teen molest, sodomy case
items.summary: District police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said initial investigations found that the automotive college had failed to check whether the suspect had a criminal record. Sarudin said police would record statements from the college's management to assist in the investigation. "The suspect is currently remanded until July 10," he said, adding that police were also awaiting medical reports.

items.title: MOHE: International students form 12.6pc of IPT enrolment and do not reduce opportunities for Malaysians
items.summary: Data from the MyMoHES system shows total enrolment at 1,264,541 students, comprising 159,138 international students (12.6 per cent) and 1,105,403 Malaysian students (87.4 per cent). At postgraduate level, enrolment totals 114,601 students, comprising 75,929 Malaysians (66.26 per cent) and 38,672 international students (33.74 per cent). Of this, 145,727 (78 per cent) are local students and 41,097 (22 per cent) are international students.

items.title: Sultan Sallehuddin marks 84th birthday by conferring top honours on military and police chiefs
items.summary: Meanwhile, Kedah legislative assembly speaker Zubir Ahmad was conferred the Darjah Dato’ Setia Sultan Sallehuddin Kedah (D.S.S.S), which carries the title Dato’. Four individuals received the Darjah Dato’ Setia Diraja Kedah (D.S.D.K), which carries the title Dato’. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah was conferred the Darjah Dato’ Setia Pahlawan (D.D.S.P), which also carries the title Dato’.

items.title: Anwar-Onn Hafiz exchange just 'check and balance', not a quarrel, says Tok Mat
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has dismissed suggestions that a public exchange between Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi amounted to a quarrel, describing it as a normal part of campaigning. I do not see it as a quarrel," said Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president. "Datuk Seri Najib's position is a federal matter.

items.title: Strong westerly winds expected to hit Sabah, Sarawak and the peninsula from Wednesday, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) is forecasting a concentration of strong westerly winds that could potentially bring bad weather in several areas starting this Wednesday. The bad weather is also expected to hit the northern and eastern areas of Peninsular Malaysia on July 10 to 12, 2026. According to MetMalaysia, the situation could result in strong winds and rough seas in the South China Sea, Phuket waters and north of the Strait of Malacca.

items.title: MOF: Nearly 200,000 pickup and 4WD owners receive additional 100 litres under BUDI Diesel
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — Nearly 200,000 Malaysians who own diesel pickup trucks and 4WD vehicles have been approved for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month under the BUDI Madani Diesel programme, raising their monthly eligibility to 300 litres starting July. The ministry said recipients who use diesel vehicles owned by close family members may apply to transfer eligibility through the BUDI Madani portal, subject to verification. The ministry said BUDI Diesel uses MyKad verification to ensure subsidies are channelled only to eligible Malaysians, while non‑citizens and ineligible parties must purchase diesel at unsubsidised prices.

items.title: Youth must choose leaders who secure their future, not race politics, says Anwar
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on young people to reject divisive race-based politics and instead use the upcoming Johor state election to elect leaders committed to safeguarding their future. Anwar, who is also Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, said young voters should make their choice based on a leader’s ability to address issues that directly affect people’s lives, such as education, employment opportunities and the state’s development, rather than being swayed by racial sentiments. Also present were PH candidate for the Simpang Jeram state seat Nazri Abd Rahman and PH candidate for the Bukit Naning state seat Md Ysahrudin Kusni.

items.title: Johor campaign billboards torn down in Bukit Batu, Pakatan hopeful Arthur Chiong cries sabotage
items.summary: KULAI, July 5 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Bukit Batu state constituency Arthur Chiong Sen Sern expressed regret over the actions of irresponsible parties who committed sabotage and treacherous acts against his campaign materials. The Bukit Batu seat will witness a five-cornered contest involving Arthur (PH-PKR), R. Kumaran (BN-MIC), M. Premanand (Muda), G. Tamili (Bersama) as well as independent candidate Kamaruzaman Ali. N51 Bukit Batu: 49,963 voters R. Kumaran (BN-MIC) Kamaruzaman Ali (Independent/IND) G. Tamili (Together) Arthur Chiong Sen Sern (PH-PKR) M. Premanand (Muda) (2022: Chiong Sen Sern: PKR: Majority 137) — Bernama

items.title: TVET Commission Bill expected to reach Parliament by end of the year, says Zahid
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the commission would be set up to replace the National TVET Council as part of efforts to further strengthen the country’s TVET ecosystem. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said extensive engagement sessions with various stakeholders were currently underway before the next steps could be taken. Also present were Deputy Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Rubiah Wang and Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.

items.title: ‘I do not promise the moon and stars’: Pakatan hopeful for Bukit Naning pledges free ambulance, better internet and street lights
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — “I do not promise the moon and stars, but I promise to work with the people to resolve outstanding issues,” said Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Bukit Naning state constituency, Md Ysahrudin Kusni. Holding to realistic principles, Md Ysahrudin, who was the former incumbent of the seat, placed the provision of free ambulance services as one of his five main pledges to ease the burden on residents if given a mandate in the Johor state election. Md Ysahrudin, 47, said the facility is very important due to Bukit Naning’s location being far from Muar Hospital and Batu Pahat Hospital, in addition to the constraints of ambulances at existing health clinics, which are often tied up with other emergency duties.

items.title: Langkawi should forge its own identity, not copy Phuket or Bali, says Kedah Sultan
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 5 — The development of Langkawi Island should be shaped according to its own identity and character, without imitating or comparing itself to other tourist destinations such as Phuket, Thailand, or Bali, Indonesia, the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, said. His Royal Highness said Langkawi Island was a strategic asset to Kedah and the nation, serving as a premier tourist destination and a key driver of the state’s economic growth. Also present were the Sultanah of Kedah, Sultanah Maliha Tengku Ariff; Raja Muda of Kedah Tengku Sarafudin Badlishah Sultan Sallehuddin; Raja Puan Muda of Kedah Che Puan Muda Zaheeda Mohamad Ariff; Tunku Mahkota of Kedah Tunku Shazuddin Ariff Sultan Sallehuddin; and Tunku Puan Mahkota of Kedah Che Puan Nur Julie Ariff.

items.title: King and PM extend birthday wishes to Kedah Sultan on his 84th birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today extended birthday greetings to the Sultan of Kedah, Al-Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, on the occasion of His Royal Highness’ 84th birthday. In a post on the official Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty prayed that Sultan Sallehuddin would be blessed with happiness, good health and a long life by Allah SWT. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim also conveyed his heartfelt birthday wishes to Sultan Sallehuddin.

items.title: Man killed, another seriously injured after motorcycles collide at traffic‑light junction in Sungai Petani
items.summary: Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said initial investigations showed the crash involved two motorcycles at the junction. Police said the victim’s body was sent to the Forensic Unit of Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital for a post‑mortem and both motorcycles were taken to the police station to assist investigations. He urged witnesses or anyone with information to contact the Kuala Muda district police traffic investigation officer or visit the nearest police station.

items.title: From dondang sayang to silat, eight Malaysian heritage arts now recognised by Unesco
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH, July 5 — Eight Malaysian cultural arts have received recognition from the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (Unesco) since 2014, reflecting international appreciation of the country’s heritage. National Department for Culture and Arts (JKKN) director-general Mohd Amran Mohd Haris said the achievement was the result of close cooperation between the National Heritage Department and JKKN in preserving and elevating traditional arts on the global stage. He said this helps preserve traditional arts while ensuring they are practised appropriately.

items.title: Malaysia’s first World Rural Development Day highlights efforts to uplift rural communities, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 5 — Malaysia will celebrate the World Rural Development Day (WRDD) for the first time at Tun Abdul Razak Stadium, Jengka near Maran, tomorrow. Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Rural and Regional Development Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi is scheduled to officiate the celebration. The Ministry of Rural and Regional Development (KKDW) announced in a post on its Facebook page that the celebration will emphasise three main thrusts, namely community innovation, rural digitization and rural entrepreneur development.

items.title: Sec-gen says Pakatan’s seat-by-seat strategy is driving support in Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 5— The burgeoning support for Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election campaign is driven by the effectiveness of its campaign strategy. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said PH is implementing its campaign strategy by classifying the 56 state seats being contested according to priority levels based on current support trends on the ground. Saifuddin Nasution, who is also PKR’s joint election director, said the increase in support for PH was also aided by the opposition’s political statements that gave PH an advantage.

items.title: Stumping for Pakatan in Johor, Anwar says rivals joined forces against him because his govt is tough on corruption
items.summary: They know we safeguard everything,” he said while speaking at the PMX Meet-and-Greet programme for the Senggarang state constituency in conjunction with the Johor state election here today. Also present was PH Communications director, who is also Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said that despite making numerous visits to Johor as Prime Minister, he had never taken any land, projects or shares in the state.

items.title: Police obtain four‑day remand for 10 teens over Merotai Besar assault on student heading to Friday prayers
items.summary: The remand order, effective today until Wednesday, was issued by Magistrate Don Stiwin Malanjum to allow police to continue investigations under Section 147 of the Penal Code, according to Harian Metro . A police team from the Tawau District Police Headquarters and Bombalai Police Station arrested the suspects, aged 14 to 17, at 2pm yesterday. Police said the incident gained widespread attention after a video lasting nearly two minutes showing the assault went viral on social media.

items.title: Anwar: Govt will continue investing in Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia to reach those still missing out
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the move aims to ensure no group, especially in rural areas, is left out of govt initiatives. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the AIM microfinancing scheme is among the best initiatives of its kind globally and is difficult to match, including by Bangladesh’s Grameen Bank. He said while microfinancing models exist worldwide, AIM stands out for its significantly lower, borrower-friendly interest rates.

items.title: Immigration detains man linked to viral post alleging possession of both Malaysian and Bangladeshi passports
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Immigration Department of Malaysia has detained a Bangladeshi man in Kuantan after a viral social media post allegedly showed him holding both a Malaysian international passport and a Bangladeshi passport, even though Malaysia does not permit dual citizenship. Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said a joint task force comprising its Pahang office and the National Registration Department Pahang located the man inside a container at Jalan Kuantan Bandar Damansara on July 4. “The team found a Bangladeshi man inside the container,” he said, adding that officers also discovered two expired Malaysian international passports and a Seafarer’s Service Book belonging to a local citizen.
